experiment: Bilateral zero phase transition at sigma=1/2
description: Pattern detection on 16D commutator surrogate vectors at sigma=1/2 (critical) vs sigma!=1/2 (off-critical). Tests whether qualitative pattern structure changes at the critical line.

phase_transition_summary.qualitative_change: At sigma=1/2: pure conjugation symmetry, no bilateral zeros. Off critical: bilateral zeros appear at 95% confidence. The pattern detector sees the same qualitative transition the commutator formula predicts.

interpretation: A qualitative phase transition occurs at sigma=1/2. The bilateral zero structure collapses exactly at the critical line — consistent with the Bilateral Collapse Theorem (Phases 18-29) and empirically validating the bilateral_collapse_continuation axiom strategy.
phase_70_relevance: Core empirical signal for Phase 70 Route 1 — bilateral zero structure collapsing at sigma=1/2 is the phenomenon bilateral_collapse_continuation formalizes. The Discontinuous Gateway (SG-1 dominance at 0.994) may probe this transition analytically.
